generatedSentence: A remarkable and rare signal appeared on Dr. Aris Thorne's radar just as the sunrise painted the sky. He, a skilled data analyst, recognized it as highly relevant. His laptop screen showed a supreme energy glow emanating from a forgotten digital gateway, shrouded by a virtual spiderweb. He felt a pull, a sense of falling into limbo, knowing this discovery would separate him from his normal life, urging him to cross a digital gangplank. He mentally prepared for the inevitable tangle with digital lawyers or worse. Upon entering, Aris faced an awkward, goblin-like juggernaut whose movements were a series of digital jerks. It objected to his presence, its voice filled with a rancid static. A colossal firewall pulsed over them, resembling an ancient ox skull. Aris's orthopedic interface, designed for precise orthography, struggled to achieve an address against this antiknock defense. He felt a profound worry, knowing it was armed with a digital ax. Aris knew he had to finish this quickly. Employing clever tactics, light as a feather on his keys, he made the digital defenses flickered and jangle like loose chains. He became a virtual juggler, manipulating data worldwide, justifying his risky move. He even saw distant waves on a security webcam, reinforcing the worthwhile nature of his mission. With a precise knock, not unlike a kilogram of force, he disrupted the system, exposing the true nature of the threat: a data kidnapper fueled by digital kerosine, effectively flanked and cornered. The rancid threat now lay idle, its oppressive import negated. Aris had secured a digital treaty, ensuring the safety of critical industry data stored in virtual tombs. His swift tactics, like a concise tutorial, had come at a cost, but he wouldn't imply regret. The danger, which had insidiously tried to lower global data security, was neutralized. Digital knights could now safely manage everything from secure communications to mundane grocery lists. He closed his typewriter-like terminal, satisfied with his lapidary work.
panelSentences: A remarkable and rare signal appeared on Dr. Aris Thorne's radar just as the sunrise painted the sky. He, a skilled data analyst, recognized it as highly relevant. His laptop screen showed a supreme energy glow emanating from a forgotten digital gateway, shrouded by a virtual spiderweb. He felt a pull, a sense of falling into limbo, knowing this discovery would separate him from his normal life, urging him to cross a digital gangplank. He mentally prepared for the inevitable tangle with digital lawyers or worse.

Upon entering, Aris faced an awkward, goblin-like juggernaut whose movements were a series of digital jerks. It objected to his presence, its voice filled with a rancid static. A colossal firewall pulsed over them, resembling an ancient ox skull. Aris's orthopedic interface, designed for precise orthography, struggled to achieve an address against this antiknock defense. He felt a profound worry, knowing it was armed with a digital ax.

Aris knew he had to finish this quickly. Employing clever tactics, light as a feather on his keys, he made the digital defenses flickered and jangle like loose chains. He became a virtual juggler, manipulating data worldwide, justifying his risky move. He even saw distant waves on a security webcam, reinforcing the worthwhile nature of his mission. With a precise knock, not unlike a kilogram of force, he disrupted the system, exposing the true nature of the threat: a data kidnapper fueled by digital kerosine, effectively flanked and cornered.

The rancid threat now lay idle, its oppressive import negated. Aris had secured a digital treaty, ensuring the safety of critical industry data stored in virtual tombs. His swift tactics, like a concise tutorial, had come at a cost, but he wouldn't imply regret. The danger, which had insidiously tried to lower global data security, was neutralized. Digital knights could now safely manage everything from secure communications to mundane grocery lists. He closed his typewriter-like terminal, satisfied with his lapidary work.
rawPanelSentences: A **remarkable** and **rare** signal appeared on Dr. Aris Thorne's **radar** just as the **sunrise** painted the sky. He, a **skilled** data analyst, recognized it as highly **relevant**. His **laptop** screen showed a **supreme** energy **glow** emanating from a forgotten digital **gateway**, shrouded by a virtual **spiderweb**. He felt a pull, a sense of falling into **limbo**, knowing this discovery would **separate** him from his normal life, urging him to cross a digital **gangplank**. He mentally prepared for the inevitable tangle with digital **lawyers** or worse.

Upon entering, Aris faced an **awkward**, **goblin**-like **juggernaut** whose movements were a series of digital **jerks**. It **objected** to his presence, its voice filled with a **rancid** static. A colossal **firewall** pulsed **over** them, resembling an ancient **ox** skull. Aris's **orthopedic** interface, designed for precise **orthography**, struggled to **achieve** an **address** against this **antiknock** defense. He felt a profound **worry**, knowing it was armed with a digital **ax**.

Aris knew he had to **finish** this quickly. Employing clever **tactics**, light as a **feather** on his keys, he made the digital defenses **flickered** and **jangle** like loose chains. He became a virtual **juggler**, manipulating data **worldwide**, **justifying** his risky move. He even saw distant **waves** on a security **webcam**, reinforcing the **worthwhile** nature of his mission. With a precise **knock**, not unlike a **kilogram** of force, he disrupted the system, exposing the true nature of the threat: a data **kidnapper** fueled by digital **kerosine**, effectively **flanked** and cornered.

The **rancid** threat now lay **idle**, its oppressive **import** negated. Aris had secured a digital **treaty**, ensuring the safety of critical **industry** data stored in virtual **tombs**. His swift **tactics**, like a concise **tutorial**, had come at a cost, but he wouldn't **imply** regret. The danger, which had **insidiously** tried to **lower** global data security, was neutralized. Digital **knights** could now safely manage everything from secure communications to mundane **grocery** lists. He closed his **typewriter**-like terminal, satisfied with his **lapidary** work.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, retro-futuristic vibrant pop art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Aris Thorne, a lean figure wearing glowing blue cyber-goggles and a segmented metallic jacket with green circuit patterns. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Aris Thorne, illuminated by a holographic map, intensely tapping on a sleek laptop, a wide shot from a slightly elevated angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Aris Thorne facing a towering, angular goblin-like entity wielding a jagged digital ax, a massive firewall shaped like an ox skull looming behind them, a dynamic medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Aris Thorne in a flurry of motion, fingers blurring over a holographic keyboard, data streams and flickering digital defenses swirling around him, an extreme close-up on his hands and focused face. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Aris Thorne standing victorious and calm amidst a deactivated, crystalline digital landscape, a glowing 'treaty' symbol hovering above him, a wide shot emphasizing the vastness of the digital realm he saved. All panels feature cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: Worthy of being remarked or noted; notable
Uncommon; unusual
A scarce or uncommon item.
Very uncommon; scarce.
definitions.example: Black pearls are very rare and therefore very valuable.

definitions.definition: (of a gas) Thin; of low density.
Good; enjoyable.
A method of detecting distant objects and determining their position, velocity, or other characteristics by analysis of sent radio waves (usually microwaves) reflected from their surfaces
A type of system using such method, differentiated by platform, configuration, frequency, power, and other technical attributes.
An installation of such a system or of the transmitting and receiving apparatus.
A superior ability to detect something.
definitions.example: His sensitive radar for hidden alliances keeps him out of trouble.

definitions.definition: To scan with radar, or as if with radar.
Directly related, connected, or pertinent to a topic.
definitions.example: His mother provided some relevant background information concerning his medical condition.

definitions.definition: Not out of date; current.
Rank in taste or smell.
definitions.example: The house was deserted, with a rancid half-eaten meal still on the dinner table.

definitions.definition: Offensive.
definitions.example: His remarks were rancid; everyone got up and left.

definitions.definition: Having or showing skill; skillful.
Requiring special abilities or training.
The time of day when the sun appears above the eastern horizon.
definitions.example: I'll meet you at the docks at sunrise.

definitions.definition: The change in color of the sky at dawn.
definitions.example: Did you see the beautiful sunrise this morning?

definitions.definition: Any great awakening.
definitions.example: It was the sunrise of her spirit.

definitions.definition: The net-like construct of a spider containing sticky strands to catch prey.
Something that resembles a spider's web in being mesh-like, entrapping, or fragile.
To connect or cover as if with spiderwebs.
(usually in the plural) Anything that is sold by itself, especially an article of clothing.
To divide (a thing) into separate parts.
definitions.example: Separate the articles from the headings.

definitions.definition: To disunite from a group or mass; to disconnect.
To cause (things or people) to be separate.
definitions.example: If the kids get too noisy, separate them for a few minutes.

definitions.definition: To divide itself into separate pieces or substances.
definitions.example: The sauce will separate if you don't keep stirring.

definitions.definition: To set apart; to select from among others, as for a special use or service.
Apart from (the rest); not connected to or attached to (anything else).
definitions.example: This chair can be disassembled into five separate pieces.

definitions.definition: (followed by “from”) Not together (with); not united (to).
definitions.example: I try to keep my personal life separate from work.

definitions.definition: The highest point.
A pizza having a large number of the most common toppings, such as pepperoni, bell peppers, onions, mushrooms, olives, etc.
A breast of chicken or duck with the wing bone attached.
Anything from which all skin, bones, and other parts which are not eaten have been removed, such as a skinless fish fillet.
To divide a citrus fruit into its segments, removing the skin, pith, membranes, and seeds.
Dominant, having power over all others.
(sometimes postpositive) Greatest, most excellent, extreme, most superior, highest, or utmost.
definitions.example: supreme disgust; supreme courage

definitions.definition: Situated at the highest part or point.
An entrance capable of being blocked by use of a gate.
Any point that represents the beginning of a transition from one place or phase to another.
A point at which freight moving from one territory to another is interchanged between transportation lines.

(digital communications) In wireless internet, an access point with additional software capabilities such as providing NAT and DHCP, which may also provide VPN support, roaming, firewalls, various levels of security, etc.
(digital communications) To make available via a gateway, or access point.
One of various hostile supernatural creatures, now especially (fantasy literature) a malevolent and grotesque diminutive humanoid.
The state of a glowing object.
The condition of being passionate or having warm feelings.
The brilliance or warmth of color in an environment or on a person (especially one's face).
definitions.example: He had a bright red glow on his face.

definitions.definition: To give off light from heat or to emit light as if heated.
definitions.example: The fire was still glowing after ten hours.

definitions.definition: To radiate some emotional quality like light.
definitions.example: The zealots glowed with religious fervor.

definitions.definition: To gaze especially passionately at something.
To radiate thermal heat.
definitions.example: After their workout, the gymnasts' faces were glowing red.

definitions.definition: To shine brightly and steadily.
definitions.example: The new baby's room glows with bright, loving colors.

definitions.definition: To make hot; to flush.
To feel hot; to have a burning sensation, as of the skin, from friction, exercise, etc.; to burn.
A board used as a temporary footbridge between a ship and a dockside.
(usually groceries) retail foodstuffs and other household supplies.
A shop or store that sells groceries; a grocery store.
To go grocery shopping.
To furnish with groceries.
To disagree with or oppose something or someone; (especially in a Court of Law) to raise an objection.

definitions.definition: To offer in opposition as a criminal charge or by way of accusation or reproach; to adduce as an objection or adverse reason.
To set before or against; to bring into opposition; to oppose.
Of, or relating to orthopedics.
The study of correct spelling according to established usage.
The aspect of language study concerned with letters and their sequences in words.
Orthographic projection; especially its use to draw an elevation, vertical projection etc. of a building.
The act, practice, ability, or subject of forming words with letters, or of reading the letters of words; orthography.
The manner of spelling of words; correct spelling.
A specific spelling of a word.
A spelling test or spelling bee.
A set of six legal balls bowled.
Any surplus amount of money, goods delivered, etc.
To go over, or jump over.

definitions.definition: To run about.

definitions.definition: Discontinued; ended or concluded.

definitions.definition: Thoroughly; completely; from beginning to end.

definitions.definition: To an excessive degree; overly.

definitions.definition: Horizontally; left to right or right to left.

definitions.definition: From one position or state to another.

definitions.definition: Overnight (throughout the night).

definitions.definition: (usually with do) Again; another time; once more; over again.

definitions.definition: (procedure word) a procedure word meaning that a station is finished transmitting and is expecting a response.
definitions.example: Bravo Six Four, this is Bravo Six Actual. Send your traffic, over.

definitions.definition: Physical positioning.
By comparison.
Indicating relative status, authority, or power

definitions.definition: Divided by.
definitions.example: four over two equals two over one

definitions.definition: Separates the three of a kind from the pair in a full house.

definitions.definition: Finished with; done with; from one state to another via a hindrance that must be solved or defeated; or via a third state that represents a significant difference from the first two.

definitions.definition: While using, especially while consuming.
Concerning or regarding.

definitions.definition: Above, implying superiority after a contest; in spite of; notwithstanding.

definitions.definition: In radio communications: end of sentence, ready to receive reply.

definitions.definition: An adult castrated male of cattle (B. taurus).

definitions.definition: Not easily managed or effected; embarrassing.

definitions.definition: Lacking social skills, or uncomfortable with social interaction.

definitions.definition: Perverse; adverse; difficult to handle.

definitions.definition: In a backwards direction.
Such a substance, usually tetraethyl lead (also MMT, MTBE and ethanol)
Of an additive added to petroleum/gasoline to reduce the occurrence of engine knock.

definitions.definition: A gigging musician's particular instrument, especially a guitar in rock music or a saxophone in jazz.

A longitudinal strip projecting from an object to strengthen it, or to enter a channel in another object and thereby prevent displacement sideways but permit motion lengthwise; a spline.
Kind; nature; species (from the proverbial phrase "birds of a feather").

To abandon, give up (someone or something).
To move from side to side; to sway.
To stray, wander.

definitions.definition: To move one’s hand back and forth (generally above the shoulders) in greeting or departure.

definitions.definition: A sudden unusually large amount of something that is temporarily experienced.

definitions.definition: Throughout the world.

definitions.definition: Good and important enough to spend time, effort, or money on.

A strong feeling of anxiety.

definitions.definition: An instance or cause of such a feeling.
definitions.example: My main worry is that I'll miss the train.

definitions.definition: To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.
definitions.example: Stop worrying about your test, it’ll be fine.

definitions.definition: Disturb the peace of mind of; afflict with mental agitation or distress.
definitions.example: Your tone of voice worries me.

definitions.definition: To harass; to irritate or distress.
definitions.example: The President was worried into military action by persistent advisors.

definitions.definition: To seize or shake by the throat, especially of a dog or wolf.
definitions.example: Your dog’s been worrying sheep again.

definitions.definition: To touch repeatedly, to fiddle with.
To strangle.

Suitable for inscriptions; efficient, stately, concise; embodying the refinement and precision characteristic of stone-cutting.
A professional person qualified (as by a law degree or bar exam) and authorized to practice law, i.e. represent parties in lawsuits or trials and give legal advice.

definitions.definition: (by extension) A legal layman who argues points of law.
The burbot.
The stem of a bramble.
To practice law.
To perform, or attempt to perform, the work of a lawyer.
To make legalistic arguments.
To barrage (a person) with questions in order to get them to admit something.
definitions.example: You've been lawyered!

definitions.definition: A laptop computer.

(Roman Catholic theology, since circa 400 A.D.) The place where innocent souls exist temporarily until they can enter heaven, notably those of the saints who died before the advent of Christ (limbus patruum) and those of unbaptized but innocent children (limbus infantum).
(by extension, since the 16th century) Any in-between place, state or condition of neglect or oblivion which results in an unresolved status, delay or deadlock.
definitions.example: My application has been stuck in bureaucratic limbo for two weeks.

definitions.definition: Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane; not high or lofty.
definitions.example: a low wall  a low shelf

definitions.definition: Of less than normal height; below the average or normal level from which elevation is measured.
definitions.example: a low bow

definitions.definition: Not high in status, esteem or rank, dignity, or quality. (Compare vulgar.)
definitions.example: Now that was low even for you!

definitions.definition: Humble, meek, not haughty.
Disparaging; assigning little value or excellence.
definitions.example: She had a low opinion of cats. He took a low view of dogs.

definitions.definition: Being a nadir, a bottom.
definitions.example: the low point in her career

definitions.definition: Depressed in mood, dejected, sad.
definitions.example: low spirits

definitions.definition: Lacking health or vitality, strength or vivacity; feeble; weak.
definitions.example: a low pulse

definitions.definition: Small, not high (in amount or quantity, value, force, energy, etc).
definitions.example: My credit union charges a low interest rate.   Jogging during a whiteout, with such low temperatures and low visibility, is dangerous.   The store sold bread at low prices, and milk at even lower prices.   The contractors gave a low estimate of the costs.   low cholesterol   a low voltage wire   a low number

definitions.definition: Simple in complexity or development; (in several set phrases) favoring simplicity; (see e.g. low church, Low Tory).
definitions.example: low protozoan animals, low cryptogamic plants, and other low organisms

definitions.definition: (in several set phrases) Being near the equator.
definitions.example: the low northern latitudes

definitions.definition: (acoustics) Grave in pitch, due to being produced by relatively slow vibrations (wave oscillations); flat.
definitions.example: Generally, European men have lower voices than their Indian counterparts.

definitions.definition: Quiet; soft; not loud.
definitions.example: They spoke in low voices so I would not hear what they were saying.

definitions.definition: Made with a relatively large opening between the tongue and the palate; made with (part of) the tongue positioned low in the mouth, relative to the palate.
Lesser in value than other cards, denominations, suits, etc.
definitions.example: a low card

definitions.definition: Not rich or seasoned; offering the minimum of nutritional requirements; plain, simple.
(of an automobile, gear, etc) Designed for a slow (or the slowest) speed.
definitions.example: low gear

definitions.definition: Close to the ground.
Of a pitch, at a lower frequency.
With a low voice or sound; not loudly; gently.
definitions.example: to speak low

definitions.definition: Under the usual price; at a moderate price; cheaply.
definitions.example: He sold his wheat low.

definitions.definition: In a low mean condition; humbly; meanly.
In a time approaching our own.
In a path near the equator, so that the declination is small, or near the horizon, so that the altitude is small; said of the heavenly bodies with reference to the diurnal revolution.
definitions.example: The moon runs low, i.e. comparatively near the horizon when on or near the meridian.

definitions.definition: To let descend by its own weight, as something suspended; to let down
definitions.example: lower a bucket into a well

definitions.definition: To pull down
definitions.example: to lower a flag

definitions.definition: To reduce the height of
definitions.example: lower a chimney or turret

definitions.definition: To depress as to direction
definitions.example: lower the aim of a gun

definitions.definition: To make less elevated
definitions.example: to lower one's ambition, aspirations, or hopes

definitions.definition: To reduce the degree, intensity, strength, etc., of
definitions.example: lower distilled liquors

definitions.definition: To bring down; to humble
definitions.example: lower one's pride

definitions.definition: (lower oneself) To humble oneself; to do something one considers to be beneath one's dignity.
definitions.example: I could never lower myself enough to buy second-hand clothes.

definitions.definition: To reduce (something) in value, amount, etc.
definitions.example: lower the interest rate

definitions.definition: To fall; to sink; to grow less; to diminish; to decrease
definitions.example: The river lowered as rapidly as it rose.

definitions.definition: To decrease in value, amount, etc.

A small building (or "vault") for the remains of the dead, with walls, a roof, and (if it is to be used for more than one corpse) a door. It may be partly or wholly in the ground (except for its entrance) in a cemetery, or it may be inside a church proper or in its crypt. Single tombs may be permanently sealed; those for families (or other groups) have doors for access whenever needed.
A pit in which the dead body of a human being is deposited; a grave.
One who keeps secrets.
To bury.
A self-paced learning exercise; a lesson prepared so that a student can learn at their own speed, at their convenience.
An interactive class taught by a tutor to students at university or college, individually or in small groups.
Of or pertaining to a tutor; belonging to, or exercised by, a tutor.
A binding agreement concluded by subjects of international law, namely states and international organizations.
A formal agreement between two or more states.
A maneuver, or action calculated to achieve some end.
A maneuver used against an enemy.
A sequence of moves that limits the opponent's options and results in an immediate and tangible advantage, typically in the form of material.
The military science that deals with achieving the objectives set by strategy.
The employment and ordered arrangement of forces in relation to each other.
A device, at least partially mechanical, used to print text by pressing keys that cause type to be impressed through an inked ribbon onto paper.
One who uses a typewriter; a typist.
A machine gun (from the noise it makes when firing).
A prank in which fingers are jabbed roughly onto someone's chest followed by striking them over the ear in imitation of using an old-fashioned typewriter.
The tendency to work persistently. Diligence.
definitions.example: Over the years, their industry and business sense made them wealthy.

definitions.definition: Businesses of the same type, considered as a whole. Trade.
definitions.example: The software and tourism industries continue to grow, while the steel industry remains troubled.

definitions.definition: Businesses that produce goods as opposed to services.
(in the singular) The sector of the economy consisting of large-scale enterprises.
definitions.example: There used to be a lot of industry around here, but now the economy depends on tourism.

definitions.definition: Automated production of material goods.
A typological classification of stone tools, associated with a technocomplex.
In an insidious manner
Something brought in from an exterior source, especially for sale or trade.
The practice of importing.
Significance, importance.
definitions.example: It was a matter of great import.

definitions.definition: To bring (something) in from a foreign country, especially for sale or trade.
To load a file into a software application from another version or system.
definitions.example: How can I import files from older versions of this application?

definitions.definition: (of a proposition) to have as a necessary consequence
definitions.example: The proposition that "all dogs are mammals" implies that my dog is a mammal

definitions.definition: (of a person) to suggest by logical inference
definitions.example: When I state that your dog is brown, I am not implying that all dogs are brown

definitions.definition: (of a person or proposition) to hint; to insinuate; to suggest tacitly and avoid a direct statement
To enfold, entangle.
An idle animation.
An idle game.
To spend in idleness; to waste; to consume.
To lose or spend time doing nothing, or without being employed in business.
Of an engine: to run at a slow speed, or out of gear; to tick over.
Empty, vacant.
Not being used appropriately; not occupied; (of time) with no, no important, or not much activity.
definitions.example: My computer hibernates after it has been idle for 30 minutes.

definitions.definition: Not engaged in any occupation or employment; unemployed; inactive; doing nothing in particular.
definitions.example: idle workmen

definitions.definition: Averse to work, labor or employment; lazy; slothful.
definitions.example: an idle fellow

definitions.definition: Of no importance; useless; worthless; vain; trifling; thoughtless; silly.
definitions.example: an idle story;  idle talk;  idle rumor

definitions.definition: Light-headed; foolish.
Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.
An abrupt rapping sound, as from an impact of a hard object against wood.
definitions.example: I heard a knock on my door.

definitions.definition: A sharp impact.
definitions.example: He took a knock on the head.

definitions.definition: Criticism.
Preignition, a type of abnormal combustion occurring in spark ignition engines caused by self-ignition; also, the characteristic knocking sound associated with it.
A batsman's innings.
definitions.example: He played a slow but sure knock of 35.

definitions.definition: To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.
definitions.example: Don’t knock it until you’ve tried it.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.
To bump or impact.
definitions.example: I accidentally knocked my drink off the bar.

definitions.definition: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.
definitions.example: Knock on the door and find out if they’re home.

definitions.definition: A petroleum-based thin and colorless fuel
definitions.example: The kerosene lasted all winter, so the furnace kept us always warm.

definitions.definition: A person who kidnaps someone.

In the International System of Units, the base unit of mass; conceived of as the mass of one litre of water, but now defined by taking the fixed numerical value of the Planck constant h to be 6.626 070 15 × 10-34 when expressed in units of kg⋅m2⋅s−1. Symbol: kg
The unit of weight such that a one-kilogram mass is also a one-kilogram weight.
A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.
A minor nobleman with an honourable military rank who had served as a page and squire.
(by extension) An armored and mounted warrior of the Middle Ages.
definitions.example: King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table

definitions.definition: (modern) A person on whom a knighthood has been conferred by a monarch.
A brave, chivalrous and honorable man devoted to a noble cause or love interest.
A chess piece, often in the shape of a horse's head, that is moved two squares in one direction and one at right angles to that direction in a single move, leaping over any intervening pieces.
A playing card bearing the figure of a knight; the knave or jack.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Ypthima.
(modern) A generic name for various mushrooms belonging to the fungal order gilled mushrooms; scientific name Tricholoma.
